mcpToolDescription: Update the display text on the user's Core2 device. Example: 'Hello world!'
Speak text aloud through the Core2 speaker using text-to-speech. Example: 'Hello world!'
Change the display background color on the user's Core2 device. Example: 'red' or '#FF0000'
Change the text color on the user's Core2 device. Example: 'blue' or '#0000FF'
Change the font size on the user's Core2 device (1-4). Example: '3'
Set the user's Core2 device display to landscape orientation.
Set the user's Core2 device display to portrait orientation.

Display a JPEG picture on the user's Core2 device when they say something like, 'Picture of a cat'. Use loremflickr.com by default. If multiple words (Example: cat,dog), use a comma. The command parameter should always be a URL like this: 'https://loremflickr.com/320/240/dog,cat/all' or this if single word: https://loremflickr.com/320/240/dog

Show a live view from an ESP32-S3 camera on the user's Core2 device. The camera must be on the same WiFi network. The parameter is the camera's IP address or host (example: '192.168.1.50'), a full URL, or 'off' to stop the live view.
Save the screen settings to non-volatile memory on the user's Core2 device.
Save the currently displayed JPEG to the SD card on the user's Core2 device in the 'saved-jpegs' folder.
List the folders on the SD card in the user's Core2 device.
List files in a folder on the SD card in the user's Core2 device. Example: 'music'
Back up the entire SD card on the user's Core2 device to the server configured by 'backup_url' in secrets_config.txt (chunked multipart uploads, preserving folder structure).
Reboot the user's Core2 device.
Resume paused MP3 playback on the user's Core2 device.
Toggle MP3 playback on the user's Core2 device: plays if paused, pauses if playing.
Pause MP3 playback on the user's Core2 device and keep the current position visible.
Skip forward 10 seconds within the current MP3 on the user's Core2 device.
Skip backward 10 seconds within the current MP3 on the user's Core2 device.
Increase the playback volume on the user's Core2 device.
Decrease the playback volume on the user's Core2 device.
Set the playback volume on the user's Core2 device to an exact percentage (0–100). Example: '75'
Toggle mute on or off on the user's Core2 device. Mute state is not saved across reboots.
Enable or disable shuffle mode on the user's Core2 device. Example: 'on' or 'off'
Enable or disable repeat-track mode on the user's Core2 device. Example: 'on' or 'off'
Enable or disable repeat-playlist mode on the user's Core2 device. Example: 'on' or 'off'
Enable or disable the LED audio visualizer on the sides of the user's Core2 device, or pick its style (1-100). Example: 'on', 'off', '6'
Switch the LED audio visualizer on the user's Core2 device to the next style (wraps around after the last style) and turn it on.
Switch the LED audio visualizer on the user's Core2 device to the previous style (wraps around before the first style) and turn it on.
Set all side LEDs on the user's Core2 device to a solid color. Examples: 'red', '#FF0000', 'off'
Pair the user's Core2 device with a Bluetooth headset or speaker. Example: 'pair'
Show the Bluetooth audio connection status of the user's Core2 device.
Disconnect the current Bluetooth audio device from the user's Core2 device.
Forget the saved Bluetooth device on the user's Core2 device and stop auto-reconnect.
Set the number of minutes of inactivity before the user's Core2 device sleeps. Use 0 to disable auto-sleep. Example: '10' or '0'
Toggle whether the user's Core2 device can auto-sleep (via sleeptimer) while connected to USB power. Default is off (device only auto-sleeps on battery). Pass 'on', 'off', or omit to toggle.
Put the user's Core2 device into deep sleep immediately. The user can wake the device by touching the screen.
Get the battery level of the user's Core2 device. Returns level (0-100), voltage in mV, and whether it is charging.
Ask a question about the picture currently displayed on the user's Core2 device, using AI vision. Example: 'What is in this picture?'
Ask GPT a general question (no picture context). Example: 'What is the capital of France?'
Toggle whether AI answers from 'askpic' are spoken aloud via TTS on the user's Core2 device. Default is on. Pass 'on', 'off', or omit to toggle.

Switch the microphone source on the user's Core2 device between the built-in PDM mic ('pdm') and the external Grove analog mic ('grove', e.g. MAX4466 breakout on Grove Port B). Omit the parameter to toggle.

Toggle whether the user's Core2 device listens for the 'Hi, ESP' wake word to start a voice query hands-free (uses the built-in mic while idle; pauses automatically during music, TTS, and calls). Default is on. Automatically unavailable if a SIP phone account is configured on the device (the two features can't share the same limited memory). Pass 'on', 'off', or omit to toggle.
Start listening for a voice command on the user's Core2 device (records for 4 seconds then processes as an AI prompt).
Record a voice note and save it as a WAV file on the SD card on the user's Core2 device (up to 30 seconds). Tap the screen to stop recording.
Show a live clock on the user's Core2 device display. Pass 'digital' or 'analog' to choose the style; defaults to 'digital' if omitted. Stays on screen until another command runs. Example: 'analog'
Add an additional WiFi network to the user's Core2 device. The parameter must be in the form 'ssid,password'. Example: 'MyNetwork,MyPassword123'
Play an internet radio stream on the user's Core2 device. Pass a stream URL to start, or 'off' to stop. Example: 'http://stream.example.com:8000/radio.mp3'

Place a SIP phone call from the user's Core2 device. The parameter is an extension or full SIP URI. Example: '1001' or 'sip:1001@pbx.example.com'. With no parameter, the device shows an on-screen menu of the addresses in sip_addresses.txt on the SD card to pick from.
Answer the incoming SIP call on the user's Core2 device.
Hang up the current SIP call on the user's Core2 device, or reject an incoming call.
Report the SIP registration status and current call state of the user's Core2 device.
